questions.q: Which deity is the source and presiding power of the Brahmastra?
options: Shiva
Brahma
Vishnu
Indra
questions.explanation: The Brahmastra is the weapon of Brahma the creator, said to carry the power to scorch creation itself.

questions.q: How is the Brahmastra summoned by its wielder?
options: By reciting a secret mantra
By dipping the arrow in soma
By a year-long fire sacrifice
By the touch of a guru's hand
questions.explanation: The weapon is mantra-borne: sacred syllables invoke it into an arrow or even a blade of grass.

questions.q: From which teacher did Arjuna learn the use of Brahma's weapon?
options: Parashurama
Vyasa
Indra
Drona
questions.explanation: Drona taught the weapon to Arjuna, his favorite pupil, along with its strict conditions of use.

questions.q: In the final confrontation after the war, who proved unable to withdraw his Brahma weapon?
options: Arjuna
Karna
Ashwatthama
Drona
questions.explanation: Commanded by the sages, Arjuna recalled his weapon, but Ashwatthama could not and had to bend its course instead.

questions.q: Where did Ashwatthama finally direct his unstoppable weapon?
options: At Arjuna's chariot
Into the womb of Uttara
At the city of Hastinapura
Into the waters of the Ganga
questions.explanation: Unable to recall it, Ashwatthama turned the weapon upon Uttara's womb to end the Pandava line.

questions.q: Who restored to life the stillborn child struck by the weapon in the womb?
options: Vyasa
Dhaumya
Krishna
Narada
questions.explanation: Krishna revived the child by an act of truth, one of his last great deeds in the epic.

questions.q: Which future king was struck by the Brahma weapon before he was even born?
options: Parikshit
Abhimanyu
Janamejaya
Prativindhya
questions.explanation: Parikshit, son of Abhimanyu and Uttara, was hit in the womb and delivered stillborn before Krishna revived him.

questions.q: What humble object did Ashwatthama charge with Brahma's weapon when cornered?
options: An iron dart
His father's arrow
A chariot axle
A blade of grass
questions.explanation: Having no bow at hand, he plucked a stalk of grass and infused it with the mantra of the weapon.

questions.q: Which two sages stepped between the rival weapons and ordered them withdrawn?
options: Bhrigu and Bharadwaja
Narada and Vyasa
Vasishtha and Vishvamitra
Kanva and Durvasa
questions.explanation: Narada and Vyasa placed themselves between the two blazing weapons and commanded both warriors to recall them.

questions.q: Where did the final clash of the two Brahma weapons take place?
options: Near the hermitage of Vyasa
On the field of Kurukshetra
At the gates of Hastinapura
On the banks of the Yamuna
questions.explanation: The Pandavas ran Ashwatthama down at Vyasa's riverside hermitage, where he loosed the weapon in desperation.

questions.q: Why did Karna fail to use the Brahma weapon against Arjuna at the decisive moment?
options: Indra had reclaimed the weapon from him
His charioteer Shalya refused to advance
Parashurama's curse made him forget the mantra
The sun set before he could release it
questions.explanation: Parashurama had cursed Karna to forget his weapon-lore at the hour of greatest need, and the mantra deserted him on the seventeenth day.

questions.q: What condition did Drona attach when he taught Arjuna the Brahma weapon?
options: To use it only on the war's final day
To use it only with Drona's own leave
Never to loose it within a city's walls
Never to use it against human warriors
questions.explanation: Drona warned that the weapon must never be hurled at human foes, but reserved for superhuman adversaries alone.

questions.q: What qualification did the epic say was needed to withdraw the weapon once released?
options: A life of disciplined, celibate purity
Possession of a guru's blessing token
Mastery of all four Vedas by heart
A bath in the Ganga before battle
questions.explanation: Only one of rigorous brahmacharya and pure conduct could recall it, which Arjuna possessed and Ashwatthama lacked.

questions.q: With what declared intent did Ashwatthama release his weapon at the hermitage?
options: For the glory of my father Drona
For the destruction of the Pandavas
For the ruin of Panchala's whole line
For the end of Krishna's counsel
questions.explanation: He loosed the blade of grass with the words that it be for the destruction of the Pandavas.

questions.q: What did Arjuna wish for as he loosed his own counter-weapon?
options: The death of Ashwatthama alone
Victory for the line of Pandu
The burning of the raider's gem
Only that the enemy weapon be stilled
questions.explanation: Saluting the gods and his guru, Arjuna invoked the weapon wishing no harm, only the neutralizing of his foe's missile.

questions.q: What calamity does the epic say falls on a land where the Brahma weapon strikes?
options: A century of serpent plagues
The earth swallows the wielder whole
Twelve rainless years upon the land
Eternal night over the battlefield
questions.explanation: Where the weapon falls, the rains fail for twelve years, blighting all that lives there.

questions.q: How did Karna come to possess knowledge of the Brahma weapon?
options: From Parashurama, by hiding his true birth
From Drona, beside the other Kuru princes
From his father Surya in a waking dream
From Indra, in trade for his golden armor
questions.explanation: Karna learned it from Parashurama while posing as a brahmin, and was cursed to forget it when the deception surfaced.

questions.q: Why did the sages intervene when the two weapons blazed toward each other?
options: To save Ashwatthama from Bhima's mace
To claim both weapons for the gods
To avert the scorching of all creation
To stop the war entering a nineteenth day
questions.explanation: The meeting of two Brahma weapons threatened the worlds themselves, so Narada and Vyasa commanded their withdrawal.

questions.q: By what name does Vyasa's text call the weapon that Arjuna and Ashwatthama loosed at each other?
options: The Pashupatastra
The Brahmashira
The Narayanastra
The Vaishnavastra
questions.explanation: The Sanskrit epic names it the Brahmashira, the head of Brahma, though retellings commonly call it the Brahmastra.

questions.q: From whom had Ashwatthama himself learned the Brahma weapon?
options: From Shiva at the camp gate
From Kripa, his maternal uncle
From Vyasa in the hermitage
From his father Drona
questions.explanation: Drona imparted the weapon to his son, despite knowing the dangers of his impetuous temper.

questions.q: What punishment did Krishna pronounce on Ashwatthama for striking at the unborn child?
options: A curse of three thousand years of wandering
Banishment to the northern mountains for life
Death at the next sunrise by Bhima's hand
Rebirth as a Panchala in the coming age
questions.explanation: Krishna cursed him to roam the earth for three thousand years, alone, shunned, and reeking of blood and pus.

questions.q: What did Krishna vow concerning the child struck in the womb?
options: He would avenge the child upon Drona's line
He would himself restore the dead child to life
He would grant the mother another son
He would seal the weapon away forever
questions.explanation: Krishna declared the child would be reborn alive and rule the Kurus, and he made the vow good when Parikshit was delivered stillborn.

questions.q: When ordered to withdraw his weapon, what did Ashwatthama confess?
options: That the weapon obeyed only Drona's line
That Shiva now controlled the missile's path
That he lacked the power to recall it once loosed
That he feared Arjuna would strike him unarmed
questions.explanation: He admitted he could not draw the weapon back, and could only redirect it toward another target.

questions.q: Why was the child struck by the weapon named Parikshit?
options: After the sage who blessed his birth
For the gem set on Yudhishthira's crown
After his grandfather's chariot banner
Because he was born to a perishing line
questions.explanation: He was named for being born when the Kuru race had all but perished, the sole thread of a dying dynasty.

questions.q: Which earlier duel in the epic also saw warriors hurl Brahma's weapon at each other?
options: Arjuna against Drona at Virata
Bhishma against Parashurama over Amba
Karna against Bhima at Kurukshetra
Drona against Drupada at Kampilya
questions.explanation: In their twenty-three day duel over Amba, both Bhishma and his guru Parashurama loosed the Brahma weapon.

questions.q: How does the epic describe the moment the two weapons met?
options: The worlds shook as if the end of an age had come
A great rain quenched both fires instantly
Night fell across the field at midday
The river Ganga reversed its course
questions.explanation: The colliding weapons blazed like the fire that ends a yuga, oppressing all creatures until the sages intervened.

questions.q: What purifying act did Arjuna perform before invoking his counter-weapon?
options: He circled his chariot thrice sunwise
He bound his bowstring with darbha grass
He cast off his sandals and his armor
He sipped consecrated water in rite
questions.explanation: Arjuna touched water in ritual purification, saluted gods and guru, and only then released the weapon.

questions.q: Why did the strike on Uttara's womb threaten far more than a single life?
options: Uttara carried twin sons of Abhimanyu
The blast would consume all Hastinapura
The unborn child was the Kuru line's sole heir
It would slay Uttara and her mother too
questions.explanation: With Draupadi's sons and Abhimanyu dead, the child in Uttara's womb was the only hope of the dynasty's survival.

questions.q: Which section of the epic narrates the release of the grass-stalk weapon?
options: The Aishika episode of the Sauptika Parva
The Jalapradanika of the Stri Parva
The Rajadharma of the Shanti Parva
The Gada section of the Shalya Parva
questions.explanation: The Aishika sub-book, named for the ishika reed Ashwatthama charged with the weapon, closes the Sauptika Parva.

questions.q: Why did celestial sages rebuke Drona on the war's fifteenth day?
options: For fighting past sunset against the code
For unleashing the Brahma weapon on common troops
For refusing to face Dhrishtadyumna in duel
For abandoning Duryodhana's embattled flank
questions.explanation: Sages appeared and condemned Drona for burning ordinary soldiers ignorant of celestial weapons, urging him to lay down his arms.
